cmd/jobs/billing/advancecharges.purpose: Cobra sub-command package for charge advance operations (list customers with pending charges, advance a single customer, advance all). Guards every execution path against internal.App.ChargesAutoAdvancer == nil because charges are an optional, conditionally-wired feature.

patterns.name: Nil guard for optional features
patterns.description: Check internal.App.ChargesAutoAdvancer == nil at the start of every RunE and return a descriptive error; the field is nil when charges are disabled in config.
patterns.example: if internal.App.ChargesAutoAdvancer == nil { return fmt.Errorf("charges are not enabled") }

patterns.name: internal.App singleton access
patterns.description: All service calls go through internal.App.<Field>; never construct adapters locally.

patterns.name: cmd.Context() for context propagation
patterns.description: Pass cmd.Context() to every service call; never substitute context.Background().

patterns.name: Sub-command factory functions
patterns.description: Each sub-command is a var <Name>Cmd = func() *cobra.Command factory registered in init() via Cmd.AddCommand().
patterns.example: var AllCmd = func() *cobra.Command { cmd := &cobra.Command{Use: "all", ...}; return cmd }

patterns.name: PersistentFlags on parent Cmd for shared filters
patterns.description: --namespace is registered on the parent Cmd so all sub-commands inherit it without re-registration.

key_file_guides.role: Defines parent Cmd plus ListCmd, CustomerCmd, AllCmd for charge advancement. CustomerCmd requires --namespace and exactly one CUSTOMER_ID arg.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Every RunE must check ChargesAutoAdvancer != nil first — the field is nil when billing charges are disabled, and a missing guard panics with no user-friendly message.

anti_patterns: Omitting the nil check on ChargesAutoAdvancer — panics when the charges feature is disabled
Constructing charges services locally instead of using internal.App
Registering --namespace as a persistent flag on a sub-command instead of the parent Cmd

decisions.decision: Explicit nil check for the optional ChargesAutoAdvancer field at every RunE entry
decisions.rationale: Charges are conditionally wired; Wire produces a nil pointer when disabled. The guard yields a clear user-facing error instead of a runtime panic.

code_examples.scenario: Adding a charge sub-command that requires the optional ChargesAutoAdvancer

cmd/jobs/billing/collect.purpose: Cobra sub-command package for invoice collection operations (list collectable gathering invoices, collect for a specific customer, collect all). Uses structured input types from billingworkercollect rather than positional primitives to stay compatible with service API evolution.

patterns.name: Structured input types from domain packages
patterns.description: Pass billingworkercollect.ListCollectableInvoicesInput / CollectCustomerInvoiceInput structs rather than inline primitives so required fields are compiler-enforced and the CLI survives signature changes.

patterns.name: time.Now() as default AsOf/CollectionAt
patterns.description: Collection operations default to time.Now() for the temporal boundary; expose a flag only if callers need to override.

patterns.name: StringSliceVar for multi-value filters
patterns.description: Multi-value filters (namespaces, customerIDs, invoiceIDs) use cmd.PersistentFlags().StringSliceVar, accepting comma-separated or repeated values.

patterns.name: cmd.Context() for context propagation
patterns.description: Always pass cmd.Context() to service calls; never substitute context.Background().

patterns.name: Sub-command factory functions
patterns.description: Each sub-command is a factory var registered in init() via Cmd.AddCommand(); flags are registered inside the factory body.
patterns.example: var AllCmd = func() *cobra.Command { cmd := &cobra.Command{Use: "all", ...}; cmd.PersistentFlags()...; return cmd }

key_file_guides.role: Defines parent Cmd plus ListCmd, InvoiceCmd, AllCmd for gathering-invoice collection.
key_file_guides.watch_for: InvoiceCmd hardcodes Namespace: "default" for CustomerID — known tech debt. For multi-namespace add a --namespace flag and thread it through the input struct; do not replicate the hardcode.

anti_patterns: Hardcoding namespace strings — InvoiceCmd already has this debt; do not replicate it
Constructing billing service instances locally instead of using internal.App
Passing nil CollectionAt — always supply time.Now() or a flag-driven value
Registering multi-value filter flags as StringVar instead of StringSliceVar

decisions.decision: Use billingworkercollect input structs rather than individual parameters
decisions.rationale: Input structs evolve independently of the CLI; adding fields does not require changing every call site here.

code_examples.scenario: Adding a collect sub-command with namespace and customer filters

cmd/jobs/billing/subscriptionsync.purpose: Cobra sub-command package for subscription-to-invoice sync reconciliation (list syncable subscriptions, sync all). Delegates entirely to internal.App.BillingSubscriptionReconciler using typed reconciler input structs; provides the manual recovery path for missed billing-worker Kafka events.

patterns.name: Typed reconciler input structs with embedding
patterns.description: Use reconciler.ReconcilerListSubscriptionsInput and embed it inside reconciler.ReconcilerAllInput so list and all-run filters stay structurally identical and CLI flags are reusable.
patterns.example: internal.App.BillingSubscriptionReconciler.All(cmd.Context(), reconciler.ReconcilerAllInput{ReconcilerListSubscriptionsInput: reconciler.ReconcilerListSubscriptionsInput{Namespaces: namespaces, Customers: customerIDs, Lookback: lookback}, Force: force})

patterns.name: Default lookback constant
patterns.description: Define a defaultLookback constant (24 * time.Hour) and use it as the flag default so behavior is explicit and consistent.

patterns.name: Force flag for idempotent re-runs
patterns.description: AllCmd exposes a --force (-f) bool flag mapped to ReconcilerAllInput.Force to bypass sync-state checks for manual crash recovery.
patterns.example: cmd.PersistentFlags().BoolVar(&force, "f", false, "force reconciliation (even if the sync state would not necessarily require it)")

patterns.name: Per-sub-command flag registration
patterns.description: Filter flags (namespaces, customerIDs, lookback) are registered on each sub-command via cmd.PersistentFlags(), not the parent Cmd, because they are not universally needed.

patterns.name: cmd.Context() for context propagation
patterns.description: Pass cmd.Context() to all reconciler calls; never substitute context.Background().

key_file_guides.role: Defines parent Cmd plus ListCmd and AllCmd for subscription sync; AllCmd is the primary recovery tool for missed billing-worker events.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Package-level filter vars are shared across sub-commands — avoid duplicate registration of the same var on both ListCmd and AllCmd if they execute in the same process run (last flag-parse wins).

anti_patterns: Calling reconciler methods without a lookback filter — scans all historical subscriptions
Constructing a reconciler locally instead of using internal.App.BillingSubscriptionReconciler
Omitting the --force flag on AllCmd — manual recovery runs need it to bypass sync-state guards
Passing primitives directly to the reconciler instead of the typed input structs

decisions.rationale: Keeps list and all-run filter structs structurally identical so flags are reusable; a new list filter field becomes automatically available to the all-run command.

code_examples.scenario: Adding a subscription-sync sub-command with namespace and customer filters
